what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that flags a human-illegibility problem caused by item- prefixed filenames and generic "Note" titles across Obsidian surfaces, and argues that a title-quality pass is a prerequisite before any Obsidian lens is shown.

notes: A noticeboard flag arguing that generic item IDs and 'Note' titles make Obsidian surfaces unusable and must be fixed before any demo.
questions: What exact surfaces are included in 'EVERY Obsidian surface above'?
Who is Tim and what makes his 'translate everything to human meaning' rule authoritative here?
What does a 'title-quality pass' specifically entail, and who owns it?
What is the nature of the linked parent item board://item-3480c157?
How would success of the title-quality pass be measured?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and actual content align: it is a grounded illegibility flag that calls for a title-quality pass before Obsidian surfaces are shown.
intention_signals: To elevate title and filename readability from a cosmetic concern to a blocking prerequisite so that search, graph, and Bases surfaces become presentable to Tim.
novelty: Explicitly frames metadata hygiene—meaningful titles and derived display labels—as a gating dependency for all downstream Obsidian surfaces rather than a final polish step.
contribution: A principled, file-local argument that human-meaningful titles and display labels are prerequisites for usable Obsidian surfaces, which a fused system could keep as a quality-gate rationale.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item flagging that item-prefixed filenames and generic 'Note' titles make every Obsidian surface human-illegible, and demanding a title-quality pass before any Obsidian lens is shown.
intention_for_embedding: To establish human-readable titles and display labels as a prerequisite, not a polish step, for Obsidian surfaces.
